checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture (lines 34-81) gives a labeled directed diagram showing Epic A1/A2/A3 upstream boxes (project-context.yaml, capability-registry.json, ownership config) feeding Context Projection/Registry/ownership_digest, which feed this epic's own facet-manifest.schema.json/capability-summary.schema.json, which feed the four validate-*/compare-facet-manifest-staleness scripts, which feed Epic A3's check-component-coverage as a downstream consumer, plus prose ('This feature owns the boxes labeled THIS epic only...') stating exactly how the feature integrates with existing components. Technology choices are stated: JSON Schema draft-07 contracts (data layer), stdlib-only Python master + sh/ps1 wrappers under the existing plugins/sdd-quality-loop plugin, no new plugin (Global Constraints, Components). No frontend/UI layer exists for this feature (Technical Summary, Layer Specifications table, ux-spec.md/frontend-spec.md both N/A), so no frontend technology choice applies. This is well beyond a one-sentence description naming components without interactions.

The Architecture diagram (design.md lines 36-70) is an explicit directed dependency graph: Epic A1/A2/A3 sources -> Context Projection/Registry/ownership_digest -> facet-manifest.yaml/capability-summary.yaml + their schemas (THIS epic) -> validate-*/compare-facet-manifest-staleness scripts (THIS epic) -> Epic A3's check-component-coverage (downstream consumer). Epic A3 appears twice (as an ownership-config source feeding ownership_digest/affected_components, and separately as check-component-coverage consuming the resulting facet-manifest.yaml) but these are two distinct Epic A3 artifacts in a strictly forward chain with no edge feeding back into an earlier box -- no cycle exists in the stated graph.

design.md ## Data Plan (lines 247-271) contains all three required disclosures in substance: a 'New data:' list enumerating every new schema/vendored-copy/fixture entity this feature introduces (functionally equivalent to 'Data Entities:', though not using that exact label -- a labeling variance only, not an absent field); 'Existing Data Affected: none' with explicit rationale (this feature only reads Epic A1/A2/A3's already-fixed shapes); 'Migration Strategy: none' with explicit rationale (every artifact is wholly new, nothing is migrated in place). All three sub-field intents are satisfied with cited evidence.

design.md ## API / Contract Plan (lines 272-955) is extensive for this CLI/schema-based feature: it lists every new machine-readable contract (facet-manifest.schema.json, capability-summary.schema.json, context-projection.schema.json, full JSON Schema bodies) and every new CLI 'endpoint equivalent' (validate-facet-manifest, validate-capability-summary, validate-context-projection, compare-facet-manifest-staleness), each with its exact invocation/flags (request-schema equivalent), output/diagnostic/exit-code shape (response-schema equivalent), and branch table. No existing endpoint or contract file is deprecated or breaking-changed -- stated across Data Plan ('Existing Data Affected: none... only reads their already-fixed shapes') and requirements.md Non-goals ('Modifying contracts/capability-registry.schema.json, contracts/project-context.schema.json, contracts/approval-sidecar.schema.json, or any other Epic A1/A2/A3 contract file' is explicitly out of scope).

design.md ## Security Boundaries (near end of file) states no new trust boundary, no network access, no dynamic code execution, no credential handling. security-spec.md (a normative refinement of design.md per role instructions) is materially complete: Trust Boundaries table names 5 explicit boundaries B1-B5 (canonicalizer subprocess parse, context_binding digest-binding provenance, Policy-Weakening fail-closed staleness, discovery/vendored-copy integrity, provider-neutrality) with source/destination/assets/validation columns; Authentication Flow / Authorization sections state the auth/authz mechanism at each boundary (local OS-user/filesystem boundary, no auth mechanism, with explicit rationale, plus a human-copy gate for the one protected artifact touched); Data Classification and Protection table explicitly states 'no PII, no credential, no secret' for every entity; OWASP Mapping table covers Injection, Software and Data Integrity Failures, Security Misconfiguration, Broken Access Control, Cryptographic Failures, and Identification and Authentication Failures with concrete controls and AC/TEST references.

SKIP: this feature has no frontend surface. design.md Technical Summary/Feature Type header states 'It introduces no UI, no UX surface, no new runtime service, and no generator'; the Layer Specifications table marks both UX and Frontend rows 'N/A -- no change'; ux-spec.md states 'N/A -- no change: this feature has no UX surface'; frontend-spec.md states 'N/A -- no change: this feature introduces no browser/client UI and no new runtime service' and explicitly notes there is no Component Tree/State Shape/Routes/API Client section to check for consistency against a backend contract. feature_type is recorded as 'library' (schema contracts + CLI validator/comparator scripts, no UI, no runtime service) -- one of this check's non-fullstack skip categories in substance.

design.md ## Test Strategy (lines 996-1086) names six concrete suite pairs (facet-manifest-schema, facet-manifest-semantics, capability-summary-schema, context-projection-schema, facet-manifest-staleness, facet-manifest-parity) under tests/fixtures/facet-manifest/, each enumerated by exactly which AC/REQ rows it covers -- this is simultaneously the unit scope (per-script fixture-driven checks in suites 1-4) and integration scope (facet-manifest-staleness tests cross-script CLI-contract boundaries; facet-manifest-parity tests the .py/.sh/.ps1 wrapper boundary and installed-plugin discovery boundary across all four scripts). acceptance-tests.md maps every AC-NNN to a named TEST-NNN row with an explicit Test Target (suite + fixture description), giving a concrete acceptance-verification path. Concrete planned evidence is named throughout: exact suite filenames, tests/run-all.sh/.ps1 registration, diagnostic-id tables, exact CLI invocation strings. No fullstack critical-user-journey E2E path is required since this feature is not fullstack and has no UI (Technical Summary).

Every component named in design.md's Architecture/API-Contract-Plan sections is either defined in design.md's own ## Components list (contracts/facet-manifest.schema.json, contracts/capability-summary.schema.json, contracts/context-projection.schema.json, the four validate-*/compare-facet-manifest-staleness scripts, the vendored contracts copies, the six tests/*.tests.{sh,ps1} pairs, specs/<feature>/{facet-manifest,capability-summary}.yaml) or is an already-established cross-epic component traceable to investigation.md (Context Projection/canonicalize-sdd-yaml -> INV-007/INV-008; Registry/evaluate-predicate -> INV-004/INV-005; ownership_digest/affected_components -> INV-006/INV-019; check-component-coverage -> INV-006; Epic A5 Capability Resolver, explicitly out of this feature's scope -> INV-001). No dangling/undefined component reference was found.

design.md ## ADR Change Log states 'No new ADR is proposed by this spec. Every design decision below traces to ADR-0016 (Workflow Axes Separation), ADR-0019 (Approval Sidecar Protection...), ADR-0020 (Conditional Predicate DSL), or ADR-0021 (Context Projection Staleness) -- all four already Status: Accepted.' Verified by direct filesystem listing: docs/adr/0016-workflow-axes-separation.md, docs/adr/0019-approval-sidecar-protection.md, docs/adr/0020-conditional-predicate-dsl.md, and docs/adr/0021-context-projection-staleness.md all exist on disk. This feature's own content (three new JSON Schema contracts transcribing already-Accepted ADR text, plus four validator/comparator scripts implementing an already-fixed CLI contract) introduces no new technology choice, integration pattern, or architectural departure beyond what those four Accepted ADRs already cover, so no new ADR is required.

SKIP (recorded as PASS per this check's own skip rule): verified by direct filesystem listing (`ls -d design-system domain`) that no design-system/ directory exists anywhere in this repository's root. design.md's own Design System Compliance section independently states 'Not applicable -- no UI surface,' consistent with the verified absence of any design-system/ directory to check content against.
